title: Pure erythroid leukaemia immunophenotype
question: Which immunophenotypic profile best supports pure erythroid leukaemia?
options: CD71+, Glycophorin A+, E-cadherin+, CD117 variable
correct_answer: A. CD71+, Glycophorin A+, E-cadherin+, CD117 variable
explanation: Pure erythroid leukaemia shows erythroid markers including CD71, glycophorin A and E-cadherin with absent MPO.
takeaway: Interpret flow cytometry together with morphology, genetics and WHO 5th Edition/ICC classification.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th Edition

title: Flow profile of CBFB::MYH11 AML
question: Which aberrant marker is classically associated with AML carrying CBFB::MYH11?
explanation: AML with CBFB::MYH11 commonly shows myeloid/monocytic differentiation with aberrant CD2.
takeaway: Interpret flow cytometry together with morphology, genetics and WHO 5th Edition/ICC classification.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th Edition

title: RUNX1::RUNX1T1 AML
question: Which immunophenotypic combination most strongly suggests RUNX1::RUNX1T1 AML?
correct_answer: A. CD19 with CD56 on myeloblasts
explanation: RUNX1::RUNX1T1 AML often aberrantly expresses CD19 and CD56.
takeaway: Interpret flow cytometry together with morphology, genetics and WHO 5th Edition/ICC classification.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th Edition

title: KMT2A-rearranged AML
question: Which marker is particularly useful in KMT2A-rearranged AML with monocytic differentiation?
explanation: NG2/CSPG4 expression is a recognised clue to KMT2A-rearranged AML.
takeaway: Interpret flow cytometry together with morphology, genetics and WHO 5th Edition/ICC classification.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th Edition

title: Acute basophilic leukaemia
question: Which marker best supports basophilic differentiation?
explanation: Basophilic blasts frequently express CD123 together with other basophil-associated markers.
takeaway: Interpret flow cytometry together with morphology, genetics and WHO 5th Edition/ICC classification.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th Edition
